compiled_state.title: _phase_version_bump double-bumps on interrupted --resume when amend fails and is retried manually
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: When _phase_version_bump's git commit --amend fails (e.g., test gate or review gate blocks it), state_mark_complete is never called. On the next --resume, the idempotency check fails to detect the already-bumped version because the comparison baseline (origin/main) hasn't been pushed yet, so it bumps again. Fix: the idempotency check should compare against the pre-merge SHA recorded by _state_record_merge_sha, not origin/main.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Root cause: _phase_version_bump runs bump-version.sh unconditionally; if git commit --amend fails, state isn't marked complete, and resume double-bumps. Fix: check if version file is already modified before calling bump-version.sh.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
